observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to supply the required disclosures in the notes to financial statements on the reported accounts to aid the reader’s understanding of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue a written directive compelling the Municipal Engineer to submit monthly to the Municipal Accountant and the Audit Team the duly signed Statement of work Accomplished and Certificate of Completion on completed projects, regardless if claims are submitted by the contractors or not.
We also recommended that Municipal Accountant:
Record all the on-going and completed projects in the appropriate accounts based on the status of the projects submitted by the MEO. Otherwise, disclosed accordingly in the Notes to Financial Statements;
Properly determine the useful life of each asset and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statement; and
Compute the monthly depreciation expense and prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of each PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to:
Effect an adjustment for the erroneous recording of projects to arrive at a correct Cost of Road Network related to Road Opening to Salvacion; and
Provide the correct residual value of depreciable PPEs and compute the monthly depreciation expense in accordance with COA issued accounting policies and prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of each PPE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct and the finance committee to:
Stop granting cash advances to personnel who are not duly designated as Special Disbursing Officer and to personnel who are not duly bonded for that purpose; otherwise,
Duly designate the personnel as disbursing officers, and ensure compliance with the bonding requirements; and
Demand the immediate liquidation of the ₱2,317,321.00 from the 13 concerned personnel.

the MSWDO/GAD Focal Person to strictly adhere to the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, as amended, to ensure that all programs, projects, and activities reported in the GAD Accomplishment Report are aligned with the approved GAD Plan and Budget, thereby achieving the intended objectives of the attributed funds.

remit immediately the reconciled balance of Due to GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG accounts as of December 31, 2024.

least once a week; and

on hand and cash local treasury balances to enforce remittance of collections.

under COA Circular No. 2020-06 in order to have a uniform property
identification system.

Custodian prepare and maintain the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards,

shall be paid. Otherwise, cause the reversion of the payables to the
unappropriated surplus of the general fund.

overcharged interest and request for debiting of underpaid interests. Moving

Reclassify the non-current portion of the Receivable Account for a better presentation of receivables of the LGU in the Statement of Financial Position as at the year-end reporting date;

Require a regular review, reconciliation, and monitoring of all booked receivables to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded in the books.  Analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and take necessary actions to drop them from the books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

as the Memorandum of Agreements on the fund transfers received for the BUB
projects. In no case, shall payments be made to contractors, unless they are
properly and completely documented as provided for in COA Circular No.
2012-001.

We recommend that the Local School Board be required to prepare the
Annual School Board Budget as required in Section 99 of RA 7160 and duly
supported with programs, projects and activities that are aligned with Section
100© of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01 s. 1998
and 01-B s. 2001.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to submit the
liquidation reports together with the disbursement vouchers/payrolls and other
supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant and for the latter to submit
the same to the Auditor for audit.

We recommend to submit the duly notarized MOA, dated and signed by
witnesses; require the recipient barangays to submit official receipts for the
funds received; and submit the list of recipient barangays for the fund transfer
from General Fund to MLGU-KALAHI-CIDSS Trust Fund.

We recommend that the BAC Secretariat be required to invite observers
and post the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed, with the approved
contract, in all its procurement. Likewise, strictly observe the procurement
procedures outlined in the RA 9184 and its IRR and submit the lacking
documents to support the procurement totaling P8,628,814.58.

We recommend for the submission of the documents called for in the
COA, DBM, DILG, GCC and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8,
2015 to support the cash advance for the confidential fund. Henceforth,
strictly comply with the timeframe for the submission of DV with the complete
supporting documents as provided for in the Joint Circular.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on eight (8) depository accounts of the agency for verification of the auditor, maintain subsidiary ledgers for Cash in Bank-LCCA account and reconcile the same with the general ledgers, in accordance with Sections 74 and 114 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was tasked to prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on eight (8) depository accounts of the agency for verification of the auditor, maintain subsidiary ledgers for Cash in Bank-LCCA account and reconcile the same with the general ledgers, in accordance with Sections 74 and 114 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.reason: Delayed bank statements and incomplete snapshots.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor instruct the BAC to strictly adhere to the bidding process prescribed under the IRR of RA 9184 and its IRR, proper dissemination of bid opportunities and results, conduct of strict evaluation of bids and post-qualification and complete documentation of contract documents. Likewise, submit all executed contracts within five (5) days from perfection to ensure timely review and prompt communication of audit results.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the BAC to adhere to the process prescribed under RA 9184 and its IRR, proper dissemination of bid opportunities and results, conduct of strict evaluation of bids and post-qualification and complete documentation of contract documents. Likewise, submit all executed contracts within five (5) days from perfection to ensure timely review and prompt communication of audit results.
observations.reason: Creation of BAC started only on the last quarter of CY 2014

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the lacking disbursement vouchers amounting to P1,709,052.34, and the 49 pcs. canceled checks for audit and verification. Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to render their accounts and submit the accounts and financial reports in accordance with Section 105 of PD 1445, and COA Circular Nos. 2009-006 and 2010-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was instructed to find all the missing DVs and cancelled checks. Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to render their accounts and submit the accounts and financial reports in accordance with Section 105 of PD 1445, and COA Circular Nos. 2009-006 and 2010-001.
observations.reason: Some of the lacking DVs were brought to the suppliers/contractors for signature and cancelled checks to acknowledge receipt of the checks and several others remain unsubmitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the list and details of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year within the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO and Mun. Engineer were required to prepare and submit the list of all on-going Projects/Programs for the year that are to be implemented.
observations.reason: Submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor and the concerned officials formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the Auditor. Copy furnished the DBM, the House of Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance within sixty (60) days from receipt of the AAR in compliance with law and COA regulation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was required to formulate and submit the AAPSI and Accomplished form to the Auditor.
observations.reason: Submitted AAPSI and Accomplished report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP for the unexpended previous years’ balance of the LDRRMF, and render a Report on the Utilization of the fund in the prescribed format pursuant to Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: DRRM plans for CY 2014 were made including the unexpended balance and CY 2013 Report of Utilization were submitted.
observations.reason: Finding No. 2 and CY Report of Utilization AAR submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management formulate a Solid Waste Management Plan and budget for the safe and sanitary management of solid waste generated in areas under its jurisdiction pursuant to Sections 10 and 12 of Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management prepared the Solid Waste Management Plan and the Municipal Engineer prepared the Solid Waste Management Plan for CY 2015.
observations.reason: Finding No. 3 - Management and audit recommendation. Submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management fully utilize the GAD program funds as planned and submit the lacking documents to support the disbursements totaling P609,131.62. This recommendation is without prejudice to the results of post-audit once the documents are submitted.
observations.managementAction: Out of the CY 2014 P2,508,389.85 appropriation for GAD program, a total of P1,513,717.88 or 60.35% was utilized for GAD-related activities.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents for the disbursements were not submitted for audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers as well as the original copies of cancelled checks. Henceforth, strictly observe the timely submission of accounts and financial reports pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Sections 59 & 70, NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted DVs and cancelled checks. Several others remain unsubmitted. The Municipal Treasurer to the Auditor.
observations.reason: Some of the lacking DVs and cancelled checks were submitted while several others remain unsubmitted. Lack of thorough review and proper submission of DVs by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations RA 9184, Section 4(6) of PD 1445, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Also ensure procurement of common-use supplies be coursed thru the PS Depot and documentation be completed prior to payment.
observations.managementAction: Substantial compliance was made on documentation of procurement of goods and services; procurement of common-use supplies were resorted to PS Depot but some payments were made notwithstanding incomplete supporting documents.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents for infrastructure projects were not complete; procurement of common-use supplies was not always coursed thru PS Depot; several lacking documents were noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the current year collections and deposits report be prepared and submitted by the concerned accountable officers on a monthly basis in a prescribed format to provide basis of posting to the general ledger to come up with an accurate balance to be reported to the financial statements. Also, direct all Collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 307 of RA 7160 (The Local Government Code) and Sections 26 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.
observations.managementAction: Current year collections and deposits reports were prepared and submitted by the concerned accountable officers on a monthly basis and in the prescribed format. SEF and Trust Funds were submitted. Erroneous recording in the cashbook is still recurring.
observations.reason: Some collections and deposits were submitted; however, erroneous recording in the cashbook is still recurring.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and submit the missing Memoranda of Agreement and the supporting DVs and documents for post-audit.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum of Agreement was provided while the lacking documents in disbursement vouchers remain unsubmitted.
observations.reason: Some disbursement vouchers remain unsubmitted; the lacking supporting documents for certain disbursements were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require all employees concerned to submit their Daily Time Records and other documents to support their attendance. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer should attach those documents to the payrolls.
observations.managementAction: Some employees submitted their DTRs. Municipal Treasurer to attach documents to the payrolls.
observations.reason: Full compliance was not made; some employees still did not submit their DTRs.
